name: Hanoi Beef and Rice-Noodle Soup (Pho Bac)
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Short rib plate, or 1 lb flank steak
2 large Onions, unpeeled, halved and studded with 8 whole cloves
2 oz Fresh ginger root, unpeeled, in one piece
1/4 cup Hot chili sauce (tuong ot or sriracha sauce)
1 bunch Fresh mint, separated into leaves
1 bunch Fresh Asian basil or regular fresh basil, separated into leaves
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: In order to cut the beef into paper-thin slices, freeze the pieces of meat for 30 minutes before slicing. The night before, clean the bones under cold running water and soak overnight in a pot with water to cover at room temperature. This will help loosen impurities inside the bones. When heat is applied, these impurities are released and come to the top much faster, which can be removed, producing a clear broth.
1. Place beef bones, oxtails, and short rib plate in a large stockpot. Add water to cover and bring to a boil.
2. Cook for 10 minutes. Drain.
3. Rinse pot and bones. Return bones to the pot and add 6 quarts of water. Bring to a boil. Skim surface foam and fat.
4. Continue skimming until foam ceases to rise. Add 3 quarts more water and bring to a boil. Skim off residue on top.
5. Turn heat to low and simmer. Meanwhile, char clove-studded onions, shallots, and ginger directly over gas burner or under broiler until they release fragrant odors.
6. Tie charred vegetables, star anise, and cinnamon stick in double thickness of dampened cheesecloth. Add spice bag, parsnips, and salt to simmering broth.
7. Simmer for 1 hour. Remove short rib plates. Pull meat away from bones. Reserve meat and return bones to the pot.
8. Continue simmering uncovered for 4-5 hours, adding fresh water as needed.
9. Remove bones when broth is ready. Strain broth through a double layer of dampened cheesecloth into a clean pot.
10. Add fish sauce and bring broth to boil. Reduce heat and keep at a bare simmer.
11. Boil noodles in separate pot until drained. Divide among 4 soup bowls.
12. Top noodles with sliced meats. Bring broth to rolling boil. Ladle hot broth over meat in each bowl (the boiling broth will cook raw beef instantly).
13. Garnish with scallion mixture and freshly ground black pepper. Serve onions in chili sauce and accompaniments on the side, adding as desired.
